differential_diagnoses: Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy (CAA) with recurrent inflammation (CAA-ri)
IgG4-related disease of the central nervous system
Chronic infectious meningitis (e.g., fungal or tuberculous)
next_steps: Repeat MRI of the brain with contrast including vessel wall imaging
Serum autoimmune and vasculitis panel (ANCA, ANA, SSA/SSB, IgG4 levels)
Comprehensive CSF analysis including oligoclonal bands, cytology, and infectious panels (T-SPOT.TB, fungal markers)
Whole-body PET/CT to evaluate for systemic involvement (sarcoidosis or lymphoma)

differential_diagnoses: CLIPPERS (Chronic Lymphocytic Inflammation with Pontine Perivascular Enhancement Responsive to Steroids)
IgG4-related disease of the central nervous system
next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy with Congo red staining and immunohistochemistry for amyloid-beta
MRI of the brain with contrast including vessel wall imaging and thin-slice susceptibility-weighted imaging
Whole-body PET/CT to evaluate for systemic occult malignancy or granulomatous disease
Dermatology evaluation to rule out occult melanoma
Diagnostic lumbar puncture for comprehensive cytology, flow cytometry, and inflammatory markers (e.g., CXCL13, ACE levels)
